metadata.name: Shared Assessments SIG Full Questionnaire
metadata.description: PLACEHOLDER: Full SIG questionnaire requires commercial license. This is a structural placeholder with sample questions across all domains.
metadata.licensing_note: Production use requires Shared Assessments membership and SIG license. Contact Shared Assessments at https://sharedassessments.org for licensing information.

questions.question_text: Does your organization have a documented business continuity plan (BCP) that has been approved by senior management?
questions.description: BCP should identify critical business processes, recovery time objectives (RTOs), and recovery point objectives (RPOs).

questions.question_text: How often do you conduct business continuity tests or exercises?
questions.description: Testing should include tabletop exercises, simulation tests, or full-scale tests at least annually.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a documented disaster recovery (DR) plan with defined RTOs and RPOs?
questions.description: DR plan should address system recovery procedures, backup restoration processes, and alternate site activation.

questions.question_text: What is your backup frequency and retention period for critical data?
questions.description: Backups should be performed regularly (daily recommended) with appropriate retention periods to meet business and regulatory requirements.

questions.question_text: How frequently are backup restoration procedures tested?
questions.description: Backup restoration should be tested at least quarterly to ensure data can be successfully recovered.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain an inventory of applicable regulatory and contractual obligations?
questions.description: Organization should track all applicable regulations (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, PCI DSS, DORA, NIS2) and contractual requirements.

questions.question_text: How often do you conduct internal security audits?
questions.description: Internal audits should assess compliance with policies, standards, and regulatory requirements at least annually.

questions.question_text: Do you undergo independent third-party security audits or assessments (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001)?
questions.description: Third-party audits provide independent validation of security controls and compliance.

questions.question_text: Do you have a comprehensive set of information security policies that are reviewed and updated at least annually?
questions.description: Policy framework should include acceptable use, data classification, access control, encryption, incident response, and other key policies.

questions.question_text: Do you have a process to monitor and respond to changes in applicable laws and regulations?
questions.description: Organization should track regulatory changes and update controls, policies, and procedures accordingly.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a documented security architecture that includes network segmentation and defense-in-depth principles?
questions.description: Security architecture should implement multiple layers of defense, network segmentation, and zero-trust principles.

questions.question_text: Are firewall rules reviewed and updated at least quarterly?
questions.description: Firewall rules should be reviewed regularly to remove unnecessary rules and ensure least privilege access.

questions.question_text: Do you employ intrusion detection and/or intrusion prevention systems (IDS/IPS)?
questions.description: IDS/IPS systems should monitor network traffic for malicious activity and unauthorized access attempts.

questions.question_text: If you provide wireless network access, is it secured using WPA3 or WPA2-Enterprise with strong authentication?
questions.description: Wireless networks should use strong encryption and authentication protocols, separate from production networks.

questions.question_text: Do you require VPN with multi-factor authentication for all remote access to corporate networks?
questions.description: Remote access should be secured with VPN using strong encryption (e.g., IPSec, SSL/TLS) and MFA.

questions.question_text: Do you have a data classification policy that categorizes data based on sensitivity (e.g., public, internal, confidential, restricted)?
questions.description: Data classification enables appropriate security controls based on data sensitivity and regulatory requirements.

questions.question_text: Do you have documented data retention and disposal policies that comply with applicable regulations?
questions.description: Data retention policies should specify how long different data types are retained and secure disposal procedures.

questions.question_text: Do you implement data minimization practices to collect and retain only necessary personal data?
questions.description: Organizations should collect minimum data necessary for business purposes and limit data retention periods.

questions.question_text: What methods do you use to securely dispose of data and media containing sensitive information?
questions.description: Disposal methods should include degaussing, physical destruction, or cryptographic erasure for media, and secure deletion for electronic data.

questions.question_text: Do you have data loss prevention (DLP) controls to prevent unauthorized data exfiltration?
questions.description: DLP solutions should monitor and prevent unauthorized transmission of sensitive data via email, web, removable media, etc.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct background checks for employees and contractors with access to sensitive data or systems?
questions.description: Background checks should be performed before granting access, in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

questions.question_text: Do employees and contractors sign confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements?
questions.description: All personnel should sign agreements acknowledging their responsibilities to protect organizational information.

questions.question_text: Do you have documented procedures for revoking access immediately upon employee or contractor termination?
questions.description: Access should be revoked on termination date, including physical access, logical access, credentials, and company assets.

questions.question_text: Do you have an acceptable use policy that employees and contractors must acknowledge?
questions.description: Acceptable use policy should define appropriate use of organizational systems, data, and resources.

questions.question_text: Are access permissions assigned based on job roles and responsibilities following least privilege principles?
questions.description: Users should have only the minimum access necessary to perform their job functions.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a comprehensive inventory of all information assets, including hardware, software, and data?
questions.description: Asset inventory should be maintained, regularly updated, and include asset owners, locations, and classifications.

questions.question_text: Are all information assets assigned to specific owners with defined responsibilities?
questions.description: Each asset should have an identified owner responsible for its security, maintenance, and appropriate use.

questions.question_text: Do you have procedures for secure handling, storage, and transportation of physical and electronic media?
questions.description: Media containing sensitive information should be securely stored, transported, and tracked throughout its lifecycle.

questions.question_text: Are removable media devices (USB drives, external hard drives) encrypted and controlled?
questions.description: Removable media should be encrypted, tracked, and restricted to authorized users to prevent data loss.

questions.question_text: Is sensitive information labeled according to its classification with appropriate handling instructions?
questions.description: Data labels should indicate classification level and handling requirements (e.g., Confidential, Internal Use Only).

questions.question_text: If you use virtualization, are hypervisors hardened and patched according to vendor recommendations?
questions.description: Hypervisors should follow security hardening guidelines and receive timely security updates.

questions.question_text: If you use containers (Docker, Kubernetes), do you scan container images for vulnerabilities?
questions.description: Container images should be scanned for vulnerabilities and misconfigurations before deployment.

questions.question_text: If you use infrastructure as code (IaC), do you perform security reviews and testing of IaC templates?
questions.description: IaC templates (Terraform, CloudFormation) should be reviewed for security misconfigurations and compliance.

questions.question_text: Are virtual machines properly isolated based on data sensitivity and business function?
questions.description: VMs processing sensitive data should be isolated from less critical systems using virtual network segmentation.

questions.question_text: Do you follow industry-standard hardening guidelines (e.g., CIS Benchmarks, DISA STIGs) for servers and workstations?
questions.description: Systems should be configured according to hardening standards to reduce attack surface and vulnerabilities.

questions.question_text: Is your network segmented into distinct zones (e.g., DMZ, internal, management) with controlled access between zones?
questions.description: Network segmentation limits lateral movement and contains security breaches.

questions.question_text: Do you have DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service) protection mechanisms in place?
questions.description: DDoS protection should include rate limiting, traffic filtering, and cloud-based DDoS mitigation services.

questions.question_text: Do you continuously monitor network traffic for anomalous or malicious activity?
questions.description: Network monitoring should detect unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and malware communications.

questions.question_text: Do you implement DNS security measures (e.g., DNSSEC, DNS filtering)?
questions.description: DNS security prevents DNS spoofing, cache poisoning, and blocks access to known malicious domains.

questions.question_text: Do you use a web application firewall (WAF) to protect internet-facing applications?
questions.description: WAF should protect against common web attacks including SQL injection, XSS, and OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

questions.question_text: Are physical access controls (e.g., badge readers, biometrics) in place for data centers and secure areas?
questions.description: Physical access should be restricted to authorized personnel only, with logging of entry/exit.

questions.question_text: Do you have visitor management procedures including sign-in/sign-out and escort requirements?
questions.description: Visitors to secure areas should be logged, badged, and escorted by authorized personnel.

questions.question_text: Are environmental controls (temperature, humidity, fire suppression) in place for data centers?
questions.description: Data centers require HVAC systems, fire detection/suppression, and environmental monitoring.

questions.question_text: Is video surveillance in place for critical facilities with recorded footage retained for a defined period?
questions.description: CCTV should cover entry/exit points and sensitive areas with recordings retained per policy (typically 90 days).

questions.question_text: Are servers, network equipment, and critical systems secured in locked racks or cages?
questions.description: Critical equipment should be physically secured to prevent tampering, theft, or unauthorized access.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain a publicly available privacy policy that complies with applicable regulations (GDPR, CCPA, etc.)?
questions.description: Privacy policy should describe data collection, use, sharing, retention, and individual rights.

questions.question_text: Do you obtain and document consent for processing personal data when required by law?
questions.description: Consent should be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous, with records maintained.

questions.question_text: Do you have processes to respond to data subject access requests (DSARs) within required timeframes?
questions.description: Processes should enable individuals to access, correct, delete, or port their personal data per GDPR/CCPA requirements.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct privacy impact assessments (PIAs) or data protection impact assessments (DPIAs) for high-risk processing?
questions.description: PIAs/DPIAs should assess privacy risks before implementing new systems or processes involving personal data.

questions.question_text: If you transfer personal data internationally, do you have appropriate safeguards in place (e.g., SCCs, BCRs)?
questions.description: International data transfers must comply with GDPR requirements using standard contractual clauses or other approved mechanisms.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct formal risk assessments at least annually to identify information security and operational risks?
questions.description: Risk assessments should identify threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and impact using a documented methodology.

questions.question_text: Do you have a documented risk treatment plan that addresses identified risks through mitigation, transfer, acceptance, or avoidance?
questions.description: Risk treatment plans should specify actions, owners, timelines, and resources for addressing risks.

questions.question_text: Do you continuously monitor risk indicators and review the risk register regularly?
questions.description: Risk registers should be reviewed and updated as threats, vulnerabilities, and business context change.

questions.question_text: Do you maintain cyber insurance or other risk transfer mechanisms?
questions.description: Cyber insurance can help transfer financial risks associated with data breaches, ransomware, and business interruption.

questions.question_text: Do you consume threat intelligence feeds to inform risk assessments and security monitoring?
questions.description: Threat intelligence provides information about emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and attack techniques.

questions.question_text: Do you have a documented incident response plan that is tested at least annually?
questions.description: IR plan should cover detection, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident review.

questions.question_text: Do you have a designated incident response team with defined roles and responsibilities?
questions.description: IR team should include technical, legal, communications, and management representatives with clear responsibilities.

questions.question_text: Do you have procedures to notify relevant stakeholders (customers, regulators) of security incidents within required timeframes?
questions.description: Notification procedures should comply with GDPR (72 hours), NIS2, DORA, and contractual requirements.

questions.question_text: Do you have digital forensics capabilities or access to forensics services for incident investigation?
questions.description: Forensics capabilities enable evidence collection, root cause analysis, and support legal proceedings.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct post-incident reviews to identify lessons learned and improve security controls?
questions.description: Post-incident reviews should analyze incident response effectiveness and implement improvements.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct due diligence and risk assessments before engaging third-party vendors?
questions.description: Vendor assessments should evaluate security controls, compliance, financial stability, and business continuity.

questions.question_text: Do vendor contracts include security requirements, SLAs, audit rights, and incident notification obligations?
questions.description: Contracts should define security expectations, right to audit, incident notification, data handling, and termination procedures.

questions.question_text: Do you continuously monitor third-party vendors through periodic reassessments and performance reviews?
questions.description: Vendor monitoring should include annual reassessments, security questionnaires, and performance metrics review.

questions.question_text: Do you assess and monitor the security risks of your vendors' subcontractors (fourth parties)?
questions.description: Fourth-party risk management ensures vendors' suppliers also maintain adequate security controls.

questions.question_text: Do you have documented procedures for vendor termination including data return or destruction?
questions.description: Exit procedures should ensure secure data return/destruction, access revocation, and transition to alternative providers.

questions.question_text: Do you have a 24/7 security operations center (SOC) or security monitoring capability?
questions.description: SOC should monitor security events, analyze threats, and coordinate incident response activities.

questions.question_text: Do you use a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system to aggregate and analyze security logs?
questions.description: SIEM should collect logs from all critical systems, correlate events, and generate alerts for security incidents.

questions.question_text: Do you deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) or extended detection and response (XDR) solutions?
questions.description: EDR/XDR provides advanced threat detection, investigation, and response capabilities on endpoints.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct proactive threat hunting activities to identify advanced or persistent threats?
questions.description: Threat hunting involves actively searching for indicators of compromise and anomalous behavior.

questions.question_text: Do you have automated security orchestration and response (SOAR) capabilities?
questions.description: SOAR enables automated incident triage, enrichment, and response actions to accelerate threat mitigation.

questions.question_text: Do all employees receive security awareness training at least annually?
questions.description: Training should cover phishing, social engineering, password security, data handling, and incident reporting.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct regular phishing simulation exercises to test employee awareness?
questions.description: Phishing simulations help identify users requiring additional training and measure program effectiveness.

questions.question_text: Do users with elevated privileges (administrators, developers) receive role-specific security training?
questions.description: Role-specific training should cover secure coding, privileged access management, and advanced threats.

questions.question_text: Do new employees receive security training during onboarding before accessing systems?
questions.description: Security training should be mandatory during onboarding and completed before system access is granted.

questions.question_text: Do you measure the effectiveness of security awareness training through testing or metrics?
questions.description: Training effectiveness can be measured through quizzes, phishing click rates, incident reports, and surveys.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct vulnerability scans of all systems at least quarterly?
questions.description: Vulnerability scanning should identify security weaknesses in operating systems, applications, and network devices.

questions.question_text: Do you have a documented patch management process with defined timeframes based on vulnerability severity?
questions.description: Critical patches should be applied within days, high within weeks, following testing in non-production environments.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct penetration testing by qualified professionals at least annually?
questions.description: Penetration testing simulates real-world attacks to identify exploitable vulnerabilities and security gaps.

questions.question_text: Do you track vulnerabilities through remediation with assigned owners and due dates?
questions.description: Vulnerability tracking ensures identified issues are prioritized, assigned, and resolved within defined timeframes.

questions.question_text: Do you have a vulnerability disclosure or bug bounty program for external security researchers?
questions.description: Vulnerability disclosure programs enable responsible reporting of security issues by external researchers.

questions.question_text: Do you deploy anti-malware/anti-virus software on all endpoints with real-time scanning and automatic updates?
questions.description: Endpoint protection should detect and block malware, viruses, ransomware, and other malicious software.

questions.question_text: Are all laptops, mobile devices, and removable media encrypted with full-disk or file-level encryption?
questions.description: Device encryption protects data if devices are lost, stolen, or improperly disposed of.

questions.question_text: Do you use mobile device management (MDM) to secure and manage corporate mobile devices?
questions.description: MDM enables remote configuration, policy enforcement, application management, and device wiping.

questions.question_text: If you allow bring-your-own-device (BYOD), do you have security requirements and controls for personal devices?
questions.description: BYOD policies should require encryption, MDM enrollment, passcodes, and data separation (containerization).

questions.question_text: Are endpoints hardened according to security baselines (e.g., disabling unnecessary services, restricting admin rights)?
questions.description: Endpoint hardening reduces attack surface through configuration management and least privilege enforcement.

questions.question_text: Do you use cloud security posture management (CSPM) tools to detect misconfigurations?
questions.description: CSPM tools continuously monitor cloud infrastructure for security misconfigurations and compliance violations.

questions.question_text: Do you implement identity federation and single sign-on (SSO) for cloud applications?
questions.description: Identity federation centralizes authentication and enables MFA across cloud services.

questions.question_text: If you use cloud services, do you control data residency and ensure data is stored in approved regions?
questions.description: Data residency controls ensure compliance with regulations requiring data to remain in specific jurisdictions.

questions.question_text: Do you implement cloud workload protection platform (CWPP) for runtime security of cloud workloads?
questions.description: CWPP provides vulnerability management, runtime protection, and compliance monitoring for cloud workloads.

questions.question_text: Do you understand and document the shared responsibility model for your cloud services?
questions.description: Shared responsibility model defines which security controls are managed by cloud provider vs. customer.

questions.question_text: Do you have a formal user access provisioning process with management approval?
questions.description: Access provisioning should require manager approval and follow least privilege principles.

questions.question_text: Do you conduct periodic access reviews (at least quarterly) to validate user permissions?
questions.description: Access reviews ensure users have appropriate access and identify orphaned or excessive permissions.

questions.question_text: Do you enforce strong password requirements (minimum length, complexity, expiration)?
questions.description: Password policies should require minimum 12 characters, complexity, and prevent password reuse.

questions.question_text: Do you implement privileged access management (PAM) with session recording for administrative accounts?
questions.description: PAM controls privileged access through vaulting, session management, and activity monitoring.

questions.question_text: Do you implement account lockout policies after a defined number of failed login attempts?
questions.description: Account lockout prevents brute-force attacks by temporarily disabling accounts after failed login attempts.

questions.question_text: Do you log security-relevant events including authentication, authorization, and administrative actions?
questions.description: Security logs should capture who accessed what, when, from where, and what changes were made.

questions.question_text: Are security logs retained for at least 90 days (or per regulatory requirements)?
questions.description: Log retention enables investigation of security incidents and compliance with regulations.

questions.question_text: Are security logs protected from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion?
questions.description: Log integrity controls prevent attackers from covering their tracks by modifying or deleting logs.

questions.question_text: Do you use Network Time Protocol (NTP) to synchronize system clocks across your infrastructure?
questions.description: Time synchronization ensures accurate timestamps for security events, critical for incident investigation.

questions.question_text: Do you have documented procedures for investigating and responding to security alerts?
questions.description: Alert response procedures ensure security events are triaged, investigated, and escalated appropriately.
